purpose: Low-intrusion timing profile for the rejected DeepSeek MBS8 mixed Primus-Turbo normal/cold dispatch+permute under standard-EP top-8 hot-helper.

active_path.hot_helper: standard-EP top-8 execute, owner_compact row materialization
active_path.not_active: native Primus-Turbo compact hot/cold/helper row ABI

step_2_metric.note: Timing hooks are active; use this as a profile, not a throughput promotion.

current_best_profile_reference_rankmax_m.note: Reference profile is also diagnostic/intrusive; compare direction and buckets, not absolute retained throughput.

read: The mixed path is not native hot-helper. It keeps the standard hot/cold/helper policy and helper graph, but adds Primus-Turbo raw top-k dispatch+permute/combine for normal/cold rows.

The inserted Turbo edge is visible: dispatch+permute is 162.458 ms rank-max and unpermute/combine is 201.249 ms rank-max at step 2, while standard count exchange, hot-mask planning, hot-weight movement, helper grouped MLP, and weighted scatter remain.

Because the wrapper graph is still present, this path can be slower than raw Turbo no-helper and slower than standard-EP hot-helper. The next code target should not be another raw top-k adapter; it should be native compact hot/cold/helper ABI support in Primus-Turbo.
next_target: Expose a Primus-Turbo compact-row ABI that consumes the saved owner-compact partition directly instead of rebuilding a token-major [tokens, top_k] normal-route tensor.
Keep normal/cold and helper-hot rows in one backend-owned dispatch plan so helper rows are not a source-rank sidecar.
Return compact dX/dTopK and reduce hot partial dW through the backend contract, rather than leaving weighted scatter and helper VJP as separate TorchTitan wrapper work.
